releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Changelog:
- Reorganised settings
- Added Headless mode
- Made terminal and nvim settings consistent and both use full paths
- General improvements and cleanup

- Fixed bug in how terminal binaries are found
- Small README updates

- Improvements to logging
- Better windows support

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Fixes issue with [obsidian-bridge.nvim](https://github.com/oflisback/obsidian-bridge.nvim) not being able to authenticate

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Fixes issue trying to open files using relative paths

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Dependency updates
- Fixes for macOS
- Improvements to identification of terminal binaries

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Added logging for neovim information to aid with debugging user issues

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - If no instance is found, plugin will not check for the open port incase the user is not letting the plugin manage neovim

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Stop plugin from trying to open files when neovim isn't open

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Fix bug causing multiple buffers to open due to spaces in filenames

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Improvements to process spawning

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Allows plugin to startup despite failing checks (such as incorrectly detecting no valid neovim binary)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Remove min version requirement for neovim binary
- Add setting to override path to neovim binary incase to plugin is unable to auto-detect neovim

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - The Neovim instance will now close when Obsidian exits to prevent orphan processes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Active file in Obsidian is opened in a buffer in Neovim shortly after running the `Open Neovim` cmd
- If a instance of neovim (that the plugin is keeping track of) is running when the plugin is disabled, then that instance will close.
- Minor improvements in preparation for working towards a more stable `Neovim => Obsidian` communication direction

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Added a command to open a new Neovim instance from inside

releases.author: TheseusGrey
releases.description:
Added supported file types:

- Lets users configure which files they want to open as new buffers when browsing their vault
- Supported file types can be configured through settings, comes with an initial list that hopefully covers most use cases
releases.name: v1.0.1: Add option for supported file types

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Got enough feature to consider this a `v1.0.0`:

- Can open a neovim instance in a terminal of choice on startup (configurable in plugin settings)
- Both Obsidian and the neovim instance will be pointing to the same `IP:PORT` (can change via settings)
- When opening a file in Obsidian, a corresponding buffer will open in the neovim instance.

Fair warning to users, this plugin does spawn child process, and they are all and always will be nvim commands, but that is something to bare in mind if you don't want a plugin to have that much power.
releases.name: Initial Publish
